facts.fact: The insurer is SecureLife Insurance Company.
The policy number is SL-2024-TRM-00123.
The issue date is January 15, 2024.
The policy type is 20-Year Level Term Life Insurance.
The policy owner is John Michael Anderson.
The insured person is John Michael Anderson.
John Michael Anderson's date of birth is March 22, 1985.
John Michael Anderson's age at issue is 38 years.
The death benefit amount is $500,000.
The policy term is 20 years.
The policy expires on January 15, 2044.
The premium amount is $45.00 per month.
The premium payment mode is monthly automatic deduction.
The primary beneficiary is Sarah Elizabeth Anderson (Spouse) at 100%.
The contingent beneficiary Emma Grace Anderson (Daughter) receives 50%.
The contingent beneficiary Lucas James Anderson (Son) receives 50%.
The Death Benefit is defined as the amount payable to the beneficiary upon the death of the insured during the policy term.
The Premium Due Date is defined as the 15th day of each month.
The Grace Period is defined as the 31-day period following the premium due date during which coverage continues without lapse.
The Policy Anniversary is defined as the same day and month as the issue date in each subsequent year.
The insurer will pay the death benefit to the beneficiary if the insured dies while the policy is in force.
The insurer will pay the death benefit to the beneficiary if the insurer receives proof of death acceptable to it.
The insurer will pay the death benefit to the beneficiary if the death occurs before the policy expiry date.
The death benefit will be paid in one lump sum unless the beneficiary chooses an alternate settlement option.
Premiums are due monthly on the 15th of each month.
If a premium is not paid by the due date, a grace period of 31 days is allowed.
Coverage continues during the grace period.
If the premium is not paid by the end of the grace period, the policy will lapse and coverage will terminate.
If the insured commits suicide within two years from the issue date, the insurer's liability is limited to a refund of premiums paid.
No exclusions other than the suicide clause apply to this policy.
The policy owner may convert this policy to a permanent life insurance policy without evidence of insurability.
Conversion must occur before the policy expiry date or the insured's 65th birthday, whichever is earlier.
The premium for the converted policy will be based on the insured's attained age at conversion.
The premium for the converted policy will be based on the amount of coverage converted.
The premium for the converted policy will be based on the class of permanent policy selected.
To claim the death benefit, the beneficiary must submit a completed claim form.
To claim the death benefit, the beneficiary must submit a certified copy of the death certificate.
To claim the death benefit, the beneficiary must submit the original policy document (if available).
Claims should be sent to SecureLife Insurance Company, Claims Department, P.O. Box 5000, Hartford, CT 06102.
The insurer will pay valid claims within 30 days of receiving all required documentation.
The insurer cannot contest the policy after it has been in force during the insured's lifetime for two years from the issue date.
If the age or sex of the insured has been misstated, the insurer will adjust the death benefit to what the premiums paid would have purchased at the correct age and sex.
The policy owner may assign the policy.
The insurer is not responsible for the validity of any assignment.
An assignment is not binding on the insurer until it receives written notice at its home office.
The authorized signatory is the Chief Underwriting Officer of SecureLife Insurance Company.
The policy was issued on January 15, 2024.
The relationship of the primary beneficiary Sarah Elizabeth Anderson to the insured is Spouse.
Emma Grace Anderson's relationship to the insured is Daughter.
Lucas James Anderson's relationship to the insured is Son.
The policy is a level term policy, meaning the death benefit amount does not change during the 20-year term.
The premium of $45.00 per month is level and does not change during the policy term.
The insurer's liability for suicide is limited to a refund of premiums paid (not including interest).
The death benefit will be paid in one lump sum as the default settlement method.
The conversion privilege allows conversion without evidence of insurability.
The policy type description is '20-Year Level Term Life Insurance'.
The contingent beneficiaries receive benefits only if the primary beneficiary does not survive.
The policy expires on January 15, 2044 (20 years after issue date of January 15, 2024).
The insured's age at issue is stated as 38 years, consistent with a birth date of March 22, 1985 and issue date of January 15, 2024.
The policy allows alternate settlement options other than lump sum if elected by the beneficiary.
